from pyspark.sql.functions import col, when, count

spark = SparkSession.builder.appName('GDELT Analysis').getOrCreate()

# Load the GDELT events data
df = spark.read.csv('data/gdelt/gdelt_events_sample.csv', header=True, inferSchema=True)

# Filter for rebel-related events
rebel_events = df.filter(col('actor1_type1').isin('REB', 'INS') | col('actor2_type1').isin('REB', 'INS'))

# Classify events as violent or non-violent based on quad_class

    'event_type',

)

# Aggregate the counts of violent and non-violent events
result = event_classification.groupBy('event_type').agg(count('*').alias('event_count'))

# Write the result to Parquet
result.write.mode('overwrite').parquet('outputs/gdelt/rebel_event_counts')

# Write the result to CSV

spark = SparkSession.builder.appName('GDELT Analysis').getOrCreate()

data = spark.read.csv('data/gdelt/gdelt_events_sample.csv', header=True, inferSchema=True)

business_events = data.filter(col('actor1_type1') == 'BUS')

windowed_counts = business_events.groupBy(window(col('event_date'), '1 day')).agg(count('*').alias('event_count'))

significant_spikes = windowed_counts.filter(col('event_count') > 100)

significant_spikes.write.parquet('outputs/gdelt/significant_business_spikes')

from pyspark.sql.functions import col

spark = SparkSession.builder.appName('GDELT Analysis').getOrCreate()

data = spark.read.csv('data/gdelt/gdelt_events_sample.csv', header=True)

# Filter for rebel activities
df_rebel = data.filter(
    col('actor1_type1').isin('REB', 'INS') |
    col('actor2_type1').isin('REB', 'INS')
)

non_violent_events = df_rebel.filter(col('quad_class') != 4).count()

output_df = spark.createDataFrame(results, ["Event_Type", "Count"])

# Save output to parquet
output_df.write.mode('overwrite').parquet('outputs/gdelt/')

# Save output to CSV
output_df.write.mode('overwrite').csv('outputs/gdelt_csv/')
